title: The Fake Sleep Prank
description: Shimamura pretends to be asleep to see what Adachi will do. What she gets is a lullaby—and a moment of quiet, unspoken love.
content:
While Adachi was in the bath, I had an idea. I laid out the futon and turned off the lights—a harmless little prank. I wanted to see what she'd do if she came back to find me already asleep, properly asleep, not just dozing.

I hurried to dry my hair after deciding on it, but it still held a trace of warmth, a little dampness. Pressed between my head and the pillow, it left a faint coolness against my skin. You'd better hurry, or I really will fall asleep, I thought.

I figured she'd just slip in beside me under the same covers. Still, I was curious how she'd actually react.

The shift in temperature after my bath made me drowsy, and as I waited, I drifted in and out of consciousness. Even so, I caught the sound of the door opening and forced myself awake, keeping my eyes closed. I stayed still, listening to every small sound she made.

'…Shimamura?'

Her voice came softly from the doorway. Of course—she'd come back to find me already asleep. Careful not to make a sound, she closed the door and crept closer. I expected the rustle of the covers as she slipped in beside me, but it never came.

Instead, something else reached me from above.

At first it was faint, almost unsteady, but gradually it found its shape and began to flow. A voice like a plucked string, the words indistinct. Fragile—so fragile it felt as though it might break if touched. A quiet lullaby, sung without accompaniment.

Something in it drew me in, gentle and unguarded.

It was unmistakably Adachi.

'That's not a lullaby I've ever heard.'

When I finally spoke, a small laugh flickered in the dark.

I couldn't see her, not really, but I felt like I could picture her clearly enough.

If I had to put it simply—without thinking too hard—there was only one word for it.

Love.

'I knew you were awake.'

'I wondered what you'd do if I fell asleep on you… but I didn't expect that.'

'Close call,' she murmured.

I wondered what she would have done if I'd really been asleep.

'Where'd you learn that song?'

'I don't remember… it just came out.'

Her voice faltered, as if she'd stumbled on something half-formed.

'Maybe…'

She left it there. It wasn't much of an answer, but I understood.

Maybe it was something someone had sung to her, a long time ago—back when she was small.

Following the rest of that unspoken thought, the corners of my lips softened in the dark.

'Sing a little more.'

'N-no way. If you're awake, it's… embarrassing.'

'I've already fallen asleep.'

'Th-then I don't have to…'

'If you don't, I'll just lie here awake forever.'

I let out an exaggerated sigh and waited. After a while, as if giving in, she began again.

If I drifted off like this, the morning on the other side might feel a little brighter.

The lullaby settled over me, carrying that quiet promise with it.
